title: turnera ulmifolia var. acuta
intro: Turnera ulmifolia var. acuta, also known as "Mexican damiana," is a plant species in the passifloraceae family, commonly found in Mexico and the southern United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Turnera ulmifolia var. acuta include relief from menstrual cramps, improved sleep quality, and reduced stress levels. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
